content.full_text:
CITY OF MERCED
"Gateway to Yosemite"

October 22, 1990


Mark Morodomi, Esquire
F.P.P.C.
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812

Re: Request for Letter of Opinion

Dear Mr. Morodomi:

This letter is to follow up on our telephone conversation
during which you concluded, based upon the facts presented,
that Merced's Mayor, Bruce Gabriault has a potential
conflict of interest regarding the decision to go forward
with a proposed Civic Center/Hotel project. The relevant
facts are restated herein.

The Mayor owns a T.V./Appliance business and the real
property where the business operates. The property is
approximately 1700 feet from the proposed project. The
business is governed by subsection (g) of Section 18702.2 of
your regulations, and the real property is governed by
Section (a)(3) of your regulation 18702.3.

The proposed project would take ten to twenty million
dollars to build and could involve a civic center/senior
center, multi-story public parking garage, and a privately
owned hotel. It is contemplated that the project would be
financed by Redevelopment tax increment bonds. The City
Council acts as the Redevelopment Board.

The project is located in the "downtown" central core. Only
a small percentage of the City limits is located within 2500
feet of the proposed project, and approximately 15% of the
City's businesses are located with a 2500 foot radius. The
entire redevelopment project area contains about 25% of the
City's businesses.


678 West 18th Street • Merced, California 95340

Please confirm that based upon these facts, it was your
conclusion that the decision on the proposed project could
have material financial impact on the Mayor's business and
real property and that he should not participate in any
decision to move forward on the project.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Steven F. Nord
City Attorney

SFN:mt


2
